yearlySummary.intro: The yearly summary contains one row per month with the following information:
yearlySummary.privatePax: Number of passengers on private movements
yearlySummary.privateLocal: Number of movements on private local flights
yearlySummary.privateAway: Number of movements on private non-local flights
yearlySummary.privateCircuits: Number of movements on private circuits
yearlySummary.instructionPax: Number of passengers on training flights
yearlySummary.instructionLocal: Number of movements on local training flights
yearlySummary.instructionAway: Number of movements on non-local training flights
yearlySummary.instructionCircuits: Number of movements on training circuits
yearlySummary.commercialPax: Number of passengers on commercial flights
yearlySummary.commercialLocal: Number of movements on commercial local flights

validate.immatriculation: Enter the aircraft registration. It may only contain uppercase letters and numbers.
validate.mtow: Enter the maximum takeoff weight of the aircraft (in kilograms).
validate.timeDeparture: Enter the departure time in hours and minutes (local time).
validate.timeArrival: Enter the landing time in hours and minutes (local time).
validate.locationDeparture: Enter the destination aerodrome. If the aerodrome has an ICAO code, please use it.
validate.locationArrival: Enter the origin aerodrome. If the aerodrome has an ICAO code, please use it.
validate.duration: Enter the flight duration in hours and minutes.
validate.runwayDeparture: Select the runway direction for departure.
validate.runwayArrival: Select the runway direction for landing.
validate.phone: Please enter a valid phone number.

help.q1: When do I need to record a departure and when an arrival?
help.a1: As a general rule, a departure must be recorded before every takeoff and an arrival after every landing. If the circuit area is left multiple times during a flight, a departure must be recorded for each exit and an arrival for each entry.
help.q2: How do I record a flight to another aerodrome?
help.a2: For the departure, enter your destination aerodrome and the departure route. For the arrival, enter the origin aerodrome and the arrival route.
help.q3: How do I record a local flight?
help.a3post: as the destination aerodrome, and for the arrival as the origin aerodrome.
help.a4_1pre: If you stay within the circuit area, select
help.a4_1post: as the destination aerodrome for the departure and "Circuits" as the departure route. For the arrival, also select "Circuits" as the arrival route. Set the "Number of landings" on the arrival accordingly.
help.a4_2: If you leave the circuit area and perform circuits before leaving the traffic pattern, set the "Number of landings" on the corresponding arrival accordingly.
help.a4_3: If you leave the circuit area and perform circuits on arrival, set the "Number of landings" accordingly.
help.q5: Why is the aircraft data not up to date?
help.a5pre: Aircraft data is regularly imported from the FOCA aircraft registry. Please verify the data in the FOCA aircraft registry. If the data there is correct but has not been updated in this system for an extended period, please contact us via the
help.q6: I made an error while recording. Can I correct it?
help.a6pre: Yes. Select the corresponding movement from the
help.a6post: , correct the erroneous data and save the movement again.
help.q7: I recorded a movement twice. Can I delete the duplicate entry?
help.a7pre: Yes. Select the corresponding movement from the
help.a7post: and press the "Delete" button. Please review the confirmation prompt carefully to avoid accidentally deleting the wrong movement.
help.q8: I am conducting a training flight with an instructor. Who should be recorded as the pilot?
help.a8: The student pilot should be recorded as the pilot. Additionally, note the name and phone number of the flight instructor in the remarks field.
help.q9: I have another question that is not covered on this page. Who can I contact?
help.a9pre: Contact the duty flight operations manager or send us a message via the
